data.title: VIX surge above 25 implies above-average ‘but far from perilous’ recession risk
data.description:
Societe Generale said Monday the surge in Wall Street’s volatility gauge (VIX) above 25 suggests a heightened, but not hazardous, risk of an economic recession. 

The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX) shot up to its highest since September 2022 as the S&P 500 (SP500)(NYSEARCA:VOO)(NYSEARCA:IVV) sank ~2% Monday. The moves came after U.S. President Donald Trump did not stomp out recession concerns in a weekend Fox News interview. The (VIX) surged +14% to 26.73 in late-morning trade. 

“The current level of 25+ VIX suggests around 19% probability - which is c.1.8x long-term average of 11%. In other words, equity volatility implies a higher-than-normal probability of recession,” Jitesh Kumar, derivatives strategist at Societe Generale, said in a note. “Above average, but far from perilous,” SocGen said. 

Kumar also said other economic data and financial assets currently suggest a low probability of recession.

"The manufacturing sector and short-term rates are the only two series signalling higher risk of recession - not surprising given manufacturing new orders dipped below 50 in the most recent data release,” he said, and published this chart: 

 

 

 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) dropped 3% during Monday trade, and the Dow (DJI) was off by 1%. Stocks finished lower last week.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: AppLovin shares continue to fall as Citi reiterates Buy rating
data.description:
AppLovin's (NASDAQ:APP) recent steep slide continues, with shares down 11% during early market action on Monday.

After reaching a record high of $510 on February 14, share value has retreated more than 50% since. Before this recent plunge, AppLovin shares had increased several times over in the past year.

However, despite the setback, Citi Research maintains its Buy rating and lofty $600 price target on the digital advertising and e-commerce company.

Citi contends the recent plunge is related to a "series of bearish reports that, we believe, make spurious claims about AppLovin and a broader sell-off in momentum stocks."

"Based on peer's revenue growth rates, EBITDA margins and equity values, AppLovin should be worth $550 a share," said Citi analysts, led by Jason Bazinet, in an investor note. "The prevailing equity value (of $260) suggests the market is ascribing a ~50% likelihood that AppLovin's equity is worth $0. That strikes us as remarkably high. We suspect this has less to do with the merits of the bears claims. More likely, in our view, it stems from AppLovin's rapid success, opaque business model and investor's long-held skepticism about AdTech business models."

Seeking Alpha analyst Richard Durant wrote last week that AppLovin's valuation is elevated, and it will be difficult for the stock to continue providing strong results.

"I tend to think that the stock is probably reasonably valued at the moment, although there could still be significant upside if AppLovin's dominance can be extended beyond gaming and into areas like CTV," Durant noted. "Given some of the uncertainty surrounding AppLovin's business and the broader mobile gaming industry, I think it is reasonable to question the merits of paying such a high revenue multiple, despite AppLovin's amazing financials."

data.title: Summit Midstream to buy Moonrise Midstream in $90M cash and stock deal
data.description:
Summit Midstream (NYSE:SMC) -3.2% in Monday's trading after saying it agreed to acquire Moonrise Midstream from Fundare Resources for $90M, including $70M in cash and $20M in shares, continuing its strategy of building scale through strategic, bolt-on acquisitions in key basins.

Summit Midstream (NYSE:SMC) said the deal expands its gathering and processing footprint in the DJ Basin with ~80 miles of natural gas gathering pipeline, 25 miles of crude oil gathering pipeline and 65M cf/day of additional processing capacity, expandable to ~100M cf/day.

The company said it is already operationally connected to the Moonrise system through multiple existing interconnections, and it expects to capture significant operational and commercial synergies with the combined systems.

"This acquisition not only expands our footprint and dedicated acreage in one of the most active areas of the DJ Basin but also provides incremental processing capacity that we expect to utilize in the coming years as volumes behind our legacy systems continue to grow," Summit Midstream (NYSE:SMC) Chairman, President and CEO Heath Deneke said.

data.title: Camden Property, IRT, MAA raised to Buy on lower Sunbelt supply, rent prospects
data.description:
Jefferies analyst Linda Tsai on Monday upgraded apartment REITs Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT), Independence Realty Trust (NYSE:IRT) and Mid-America Apartment Communities (NYSE:MAA), each to Buy from Hold, as supply in the Sunbelt region is set to improve from heightened levels, potentially driving a better growth outlook for the REITs.

"Heightened supply, the key headwind facing Sunbelt REITs (CPT, IRT, MAA) is coming down," Tsai wrote in a note to clients, citing a moderation in CoStar's estimates for future supply in Q1 2025 vs. Q4 2024. 

"This is consistent with the three REITs' 4Q24 earnings commentary - all point to moderating supply & improved confidence in 2H25 leasing spreads," she added.

The analyst also pointed to what she believes is conservative guidance seen across the three companies. "CPT, IRT, and MAA each have a history of guiding conservatively and beating their initial guidance, and 2025 should play out similarly."

The REITs traded higher in Monday morning trading, with (NYSE:CPT) +2.4%, (NYSE:IRT) +2.8% and (NYSE:MAA) +2.7%.

Seeking Alpha's peer comparison page assesses various metrics for the three stocks.

data.title: These consumer stocks are getting slammed as recession fears ramp up
data.description:
Recession fears have ramped up in the U.S. after the wave of tariff headlines and federal job cuts over the last few weeks sparked renewed concerns over economic growth. For his part, President Donald Trump acknowledged that the U.S. economy faces a period of transition while the global trade war plays out.

While technology stocks have led the wave of selling, certain consumer discretionary categories are also performing weak. 

Cruise line stocks Carnival (CCL) -8.2%, Royal Caribbean (RCL) -5.5%, and Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) -6.9% were sharply lower as investors pulled back on the sector.

Restaurant stocks Dutch Bros (BROS) -9.4%, Wingstop (WING) -5.5%, Domino's Pizza (DPZ) -4.7%, CAVA Grouo (CAVA) -4.6%, and Brinker International (EAT) -4.6% all swung lower in early Monday trading.

Leisure stocks Peloton Interactive (PTON) -6.9% and Planet Fitness (PLNT) -5.8% were also slammed by recession fears.

Mall names Victoria's Secret (VSCO) -10.0%, Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) -7.9%, Foot Locker (FL) -7.1%, Gap (GAP) -5.8% were also bid lower.

Specialty retailers RH (RH) -9.2%, Bath & Body Works (BBWY) -8.8%, and Leslie's (LESL) -5.5% were also notably lower amid the focus on consumer spending.

The heavyweights of retail were also slumping, including Amazon (AMZN) -3.8% and Walmart (WMT) -4.0%.

data.title: Consumers nudge up year-ahead inflation expectations; their financial outlooks deteriorate: NY Fed survey
data.description:
U.S. consumers pushed up their short-term inflation expectations slightly and became more pessimistic about their future financial situations, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's February 2025 Survey of Consumer Expectations released on Monday.

Median inflation expectations for the year ahead increased by 0.1 percentage point to 3.1%, while the outlook for the three- and five-year horizons stayed unchanged, with both at 3.0%. Furthermore, survey respondents became more certain about future inflation outcomes across all three time horizons.

While perceptions about households' current financial situations compared with a year ago were generally unchanged, year-ahead expectations worsened considerably. The share of households expecting a worse financial situation a year from now climbed to 27.4%, the highest level since November 2023.

In conjunction with the reduced expectations on consumers' own financial situations, their outlook for U.S. stock prices also deteriorated, with the mean perceived probability that U.S. stock prices will be higher 12 months from now sliding 3.3 pp to 37.0%, the lowest level since December 2023.

Consumers are expecting the U.S. unemployment rate to rise, but their perception of losing their own job was roughly stable. The mean probability that the U.S. unemployment rate will be higher one year from now jumped 5.4 pp to 39.4%, the highest print since September 2023. But the mean probability of losing one's job in the next 12 months ticked down 0.1 pp to 14.1%.

Faced with increased economic uncertainty, the respondents are more likely to hold onto the jobs they have. The mean probability of leaving one's job voluntarily in the next year fell 2.3 pp to 17.6%, its lowest reading since July 2023. The mean perceived probability of finding a job in the next three months if one's current job was lost dipped 0.3 pp to 51.2%, remaining below its trailing 12-month average of 52.5%.

The median expected growth in household income rose by 0.1 pp to 3.1%, within the series' narrow range of 2.9%-3.3% since January 2023. Meanwhile, their expectations for spending in the next year bumped up. Median nominal household spending growth rose 0.6 pp to 5.0%, moving just above its trailing 12-month average of 4.9%.

The average perceived probability of missing a minimum debt payment in the next three months climbed 1.3 pp to 14.6%, the highest level since April 2020. At the same time, they expect credit will be harder to come by. The share of respondents expecting it will be harder to obtain credit a year from now jumped to 46.7%, the highest reading since June 2024, from 35.6%.

data.title: Google-Apple search payment deal could be at risk, analysts warn
data.description:
Following last week's Department of Justice's revised remedies in its antitrust case against Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL), Wall Street is warning the lucrative deal between Google and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) over mobile search could be at risk.

“We view the DOJ’s [revised proposed final judgment] as only modestly different from the Initial Proposed Final Judgment (IPFJ) filed on 11/20/24, and therefore it does not provide the relief to Google that some investors had hoped,” J.P. Morgan analyst Doug Anmuth wrote in a note to clients. The key changes from the initial version include Choice Screens in the list of what Google can't provide payments for, including Apple, which would be a negative. 

Google reportedly paid Apple approximately $20B in 2022 to be the default search engine in Safari. In late 2023, investment firm Bernstein said the deal was likely worth around $20B to the iPhone maker.

However, the judge's final decision, which is expected in early August, is likely to be “more balanced” from what the Dept. of Justice and Google have proposed and there could be a “greater consideration” on what the impact is to consumers. “While we continue to expect some negative impact on Google’s financials upon enforcement of any remedy, determining the financial implications remains challenging due to uncertainty around the actual remedies, timing of remedy enforcement following appeal & likely injunction, & importantly the path of how Apple ultimately proceeds in search,” Anmuth explained.

If the Google-Apple deal were to go away, Apple would likely look to an alternative search provider, perhaps Microsoft (MSFT) or OpenAI. It could also look for a combination of providers, or it could build or buy its own search option, Anmuth posited.

Barclays analysts, led by Ross Sandler, said the revised judgment is “slightly worse than before,” and could lead to new entrants, such as Amazon (AMZN), Meta (META) or others getting into the game. 

“With Google’s [traffic acquisition costs] getting shut down (assuming the court selects this option), this change should close the 72% RPQ gap with Bing rather quickly, and give the 50% of search access points that Google pays for default currently, an outlet to switch to another search text ads provider,” the analysts wrote. “This would also open the door to new players like Meta or Amazon potentially getting into the space more broadly.” 

The analysts added that the best course of action for Google — which reportedly has already explored a settlement with the DoJ — could be to “preemptively settle on more favorable terms.”

data.title: Petrobras to pay $283M in U.S. settlement with EIG
data.description:
Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) -1.7% in early trading Monday after saying its board approved an agreement to settle a U.S. court dispute with EIG Energy Fund XIV, including a $283M payment by the Brazilian company.

In a Brazil securities filing, Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) said the amount of the contingency is provisioned in its earnings; the case is related to EIG's investment in FIP Sondas, a former shareholder of drillship company Sete Brasil.

Separately, Petrobras (PBR) said it has terminated the sale of its shares in fuel distributor Petrobras Colombia Combustibles.

Also, Petrobras (PBR) and partner Ecopetrol (EC) said they conducted a drill stem test at a well related to a giant natural gas discovery off the coast of Colombia.

Last year, the Brazilian company highlighted that two discoveries in Colombian waters raised the gas potential in the Guajira offshore basin to ~6T cf.

data.title: IPO Roundup: CoreWeave, Cal Redwood Acquisition and more
data.description:
Small issuers continued to dominate the U.S. IPO pipeline this week. However, all focus is on the NVIDIA-backed AI cloud services provider CoreWeave (CRWV) which recently filed for an IPO.

"The market opportunity for providing AI-related computing services is large and expected to grow at a very high rate of growth, so the company enjoys extremely strong industry growth dynamics in its favor," said SA Investing Group Leader Donovan Jones in a recent article on CoreWeave. 

However, CoreWeave faces intense competition from major technology industry companies and has high customer concentration risks, Jones added. 

Last week, Kestra Medical Technologies (KMTS) saw its stock rise by over 30% as the wearable medical device company debuted on the Nasdaq. Kestra projects roughly $202M of gross proceeds from the offering. 

Upcoming IPOs/SPACs that are expected to be priced this week

  • Eastern International (ELOG) is raising $7.2M by offering 1.6M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Logistics company.
  • Epsium Enterprise (EPSM) is raising $5.6M by offering 1.3M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Holding company for alcohol beverage trader.
  • Gates Group (GTSG) is raising $6.0M by offering 1.0M shares at a price of about $6.00. Description: Real estate company.
  • Ruanyun Edai Technology (RYET) is raising $16.9M by offering 3.8M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $5.00. Description: Holding company.
  • RedCloud Holdings (RCT) is raising $22.0M by offering 4.4M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: B2B platform for trading consumer goods.
  • Baiya International Group (BIYA) is raising $12.5M by offering 2.5M shares at a price range of about $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Offshore holding company. 
  • OMS Energy Technologies (OMSE) is raising $50.0M by offering 5.6M shares at a price range of about $8.00 and $10.00. Description: Surface wellhead systems maker.

Filing, terms, and changes

  • Cal Redwood Acquisition (CRAU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $200M in an initial public offering. Description: Blank check company.
  • ELC Group Holdings (ELCG) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $10M in an initial public offering. Description: Singapore-based staffing services firm.
  • Dune Acquisition II (IPODU) has filed with the SEC to raise up to $150M in an initial public offering. Description: SPAC targetting SaaS and AI.
  • Anew Health (AVG) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $20M in an initial public offering. The company plans to offer about 5M shares priced at about $4.00. Description: Health services provider.
  • Rich Sparkle Holdings (ANPA) has filed and set terms with the SEC to raise up to $6M in an initial public offering. The company plans to offer about 5M shares priced between $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Corporate services provider.
  • Vistek (VTEC) has announced changes to its proposed deal size for its upcoming IPO. The company now plans to offer 2.3M shares priced between $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Cable laying services provider.
  • Haoxin Holdings (HXHX) has lowered its proposed deal size for its upcoming IPO. The company now plans to offer 1.8M shares priced between $4.00 and $6.00. Description: Delivery service provider. 

Lock-up period expirations

  • Trident Digital Tech (TDTH) on 3/10/2025. Price movement over the past six months: -83.4%
  • XCH (XCHG) on 3/10/2025. Price movement over the past six months: -84.1%
  • Galaxy Payroll Group (GLXG) on 3/11/2025. Price movement over the past six months: -79.3%
  • Bicara Therapeutics (BCAX) on 3/12/2025. Price movement over the past six months: - 9.57%
  • MBX Biosciences (MBX) on 3/12/2025. Price movement over the past six months: - 40.0%

Since the start of the year, the Renaissance IPO ETF (NYSEARCA:IPO) has fallen about 9.7%, underperforming the S&P 500 index, which has fallen about 1.9%.

data.title: STMicroelectronics announces repurchase of 427,820 ordinary shares
data.description:
• STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) announced on Monday the repurchase of 427,820 ordinary shares at the weighted average purchase price per share of EUR 22.9028 and for an overall price of EUR 9.78 million.
  • The company said the purpose of these transactions was to meet obligations arising from share option programs, or other allocations of shares, to employees or to members of the administrative, management or supervisory bodies of the issuer or of an associate company.
  • Following the share buybacks detailed above, the company holds in total 16,454,331 treasury shares, which represents approximately 1.8% of the Company’s issued share capital.
  • Source: Press Release
